title: Purged rolling-origin OOF stacker with regime gates instead of global blend weights
summary: Public CV literature on dependent data warns that clustered/temporal leakage can inflate AUC.
rationale: Public CV literature on dependent data warns that clustered/temporal leakage can inflate AUC. Your current fixed-weight blending is global, but pit logic changes by regime: wet vs dry, early vs late race, and likely by season/race archetype. A leakage-aware stacker can learn where each base model is reliable.
implementation_hint: Generate OOF predictions using race-level rolling-origin or purged group splits with a small embargo around fold boundaries. Train a simple second-level logistic/isotonic stacker on OOF base predictions from XGB, CatBoost, AFT, monotone XGB, and ranker, plus gating features like `IsWetCompound`, `RaceProgressBin`, `Stint`, and `EstimatedLapsLeft`. Select models by worst-fold or year-holdout robustness, not only mean CV.
expected_effect: Best chance to convert several small orthogonal gains into a real improvement while reducing local-CV optimism.
risk: Local CV may drop when leakage is removed, and an over-flexible stacker can overfit if OOF generation is not strictly fold-pure.
